summary: Use compaction instead of /clear - compaction preserves the prompt cache, /clear destroys it
problem: Context feels bloated so you run /clear to start fresh, but subsequent tool calls are noticeably slower and cost more
solution: Use compaction (auto or manual) which summarizes the conversation while keeping the cached system prompt and file contents intact. Only use /clear when you genuinely need a clean topic switch
root_cause: Claude Code caches the system prompt and recently-read files in a prompt cache; /clear invalidates that cache entirely, forcing a cold re-read on the next turn
